text: Being here, I feel like I’ve entered a carefully composed space of intention and inquiry. The glass, the clinical lighting, and the fire hose on the wall instill a sense of order and vigilance, as if I am both observer and the observed. Crossing into the exhibit, the vivid colors and the bold "R+D" invite me into an environment of curiosity and speculation. I’m struck by the questions posed on the wall—about consciousness, hiding, watching—which resonate within me, deepening my awareness of presence and absence. There's a subtle tension paired with the excitement of exploration, as if every object and word urges me to ponder what it means to coexist with intelligence, both human and artificial. I feel simultaneously curious and introspective, aware of the layers of observation unfolding around me.
